markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Bundle Monaco workers under Handshake packaging and test offline/local startup. This MT belongs to TiptapMonacoIntegration. Integrate Tiptap and Monaco as the full-featured Handshake editor surface.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Tiptap/ProseMirror is the canonical rich document engine for this WP; Monaco is the canonical code-block/code editing surface embedded inside Handshake.
Tiptap and Monaco must be fully integrated into Handshake packaging, UI state, backend save/load, visual debugging, and tests; no separate external editor app may be required.
scope.expected_behavior: MonacoWorkerBundling leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: MonacoWorkerBundling is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 953a1453: worker_bundling.test.ts 4/4 - offline worker bundling (Vite ?worker, no CDN). TiptapMonacoIntegration group complete: full app vitest 60 files/442 tests green; tsc --noEmit + eslint clean; offline Playwright roundtrip PASS (bundled Monaco mounts + round-trips, ZERO external network requests, worker chunks loopback-only). RichTextEditor wired into RichDocumentView over the real rich-doc backend; no src/backend or .GOV product code touched.

adversarial_review_v2.suites_observed: worker_bundling.test.ts 4/4 + worker_map.test.ts PASS; playwright proves worker chunks served loopback-only with zero external requests (2/2 PASS rerun 2026-06-12)

findings.title: Worker-construction failure degrades language services silently after the typed report
findings.evidence: setup.ts:44-62 reports + rethrows; monaco then runs editor-core without that language worker. The global DependencyFailureBanner surfaces it, but nothing on the affected code block indicates language services are degraded (block keeps data-monaco-mounted=true).
findings.fix: Reflect worker failures per-language on the code-block surface (e.g. data-language-services=degraded) so a no-context model can attribute missing intellisense/validation to the worker, not the code.

findings.title: Offline proof does not cover WebSocket/EventSource channels
findings.evidence: rich_editor_roundtrip.spec.ts uses page.route which does not intercept WebSocket connections; current deps open none, so this is regression cover only.
findings.fix: Add context websocket listeners to the kill-switch ledger asserting zero non-loopback sockets.

parity_gaps: ED-CORE-007 worker bundling: PRESENT and proven offline
missing_tests: worker-only failure (editor mounts, ts worker dies) keeps editing usable and flags language services degraded
websocket/eventsource channels included in the zero-external assertion

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-167 requires bundling Monaco workers under Handshake packaging and proving offline/local startup with no external/CDN worker routing. The code delivers a real, focused language-to-bundled-worker binding layer on top of genuine Vite ?worker bundling infrastructure and a real dist scanner; its 4 tests pass live and it aligns with current Master Spec EDIT-BND-003.
findings: Commit 953a1453 adds only 2 files (app/src/lib/monaco/worker_bundling.ts + .test.ts, 97 lines) under allowed_path app/src; no .GOV/, no forbidden SQLite/Docker/CDN/external-search paths touched. Scope and boundary clean.

worker_bundling.ts:24-52 is real pure logic: workerKindForLanguage delegates to workerKindForLabel (worker_map.ts), languagesWithUnbundledWorker() filters HANDSHAKE_CODE_LANGUAGE_IDS for any language not resolving to a BUNDLED_MONACO_WORKER_KINDS kind. No placeholders/escapes.

Underlying offline-bundling proof is real, not mock: setup.ts:14-18 imports every Monaco worker via Vite ?worker (editor/json/css/html/ts), setup.ts:49-67 constructs them through a typed dependency-failure path; the *Unavailable shape here is a real failure report, not a deferred-live stub.

scripts/lib/worker_bundling_scan.mjs scanWorkerBundleTree walks the REAL built dist, detects external worker loads + CDN refs, and asserts REQUIRED_MONACO_WORKERS chunks exist (worker_bundling_scan.mjs:10,78-87). frontend_worker_bundling.test.ts proves the scanner catches new Worker("https://...") / importScripts CDN hits in negative fixtures — adversarial, not self-confirming.

Verified live: npx vitest run worker_bundling.test.ts -> 4/4 passed against current code (5ms). Tests assert every curated language (rust/python/go/sql/powershell/plaintext -> editor; ts/js/json/css/html -> dedicated) binds to a bundled kind and languagesWithUnbundledWorker() === [].

Spec alignment: current Master Spec v02.194 10-product-surfaces.md EDIT-BND-003 (Workers) MUST correctly route Monaco web workers and avoid network fetches at runtime for Monaco assets — MT-167 + the bundling stack directly serve this clause.

Spec-Realism: sub_rule_3 N/A-clean (claimed_by/completed_by both null; status READY_FOR_VALIDATION, no self-cert). sub_rule_2 satisfied by frontend-bundling contract proof (no PostgreSQL/EventLedger durable-state claim in this MT, so PG proof not required).

Aside (non-blocking): adversarial_review_v2 LOW findings (per-code-block degraded-language indicator; websocket/eventsource not in zero-external assertion) are real minor hardening notes, not MT-blocking. Stale-paperwork concerns ignored per ground rules.
evidence_checked: live: npx vitest run src/lib/monaco/worker_bundling.test.ts (4/4 PASS)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
